caseResults.validationSummary: All validation criteria are met. The search results section is visible, the input retains 'Desk Lamp', at least one matching product ('Nova Desk Lamp') is displayed, and no 'no results found' message is present.
improvements: Replace `toContainText` with `toHaveValue` when asserting the retained value of an input element. Input fields expose their content via the `value` attribute/property, not inner text, so `expect(locator).toHaveValue('Desk Lamp')` is the correct Playwright assertion. The current use of `toContainText` on an `<input>` always receives an empty string, causing a false failure even when the field is correctly populated.

Update the `expectTextWithFallback` helper to detect whether the resolved locator is an input/textarea element and branch accordingly: use `toHaveValue(value)` for form controls and `toContainText(value)` for all other elements. This makes the helper universally correct without requiring call-site changes.

Add `data-testid='search-input'` as the primary selector for the search bar (it is already present on the element per the call log) and demote the generic CSS fallbacks (`input[type='search']`, `input[placeholder*='Search']`, `input[name='search']`) to secondary options. This eliminates ambiguous multi-selector `.first()` resolution and improves selector stability.

After filling the search field with `page.fill(selector, 'Desk Lamp')`, add an explicit assertion `await expect(page.getByTestId('search-input')).toHaveValue('Desk Lamp')` before proceeding to submit, so fill-confirmation failures are caught early and separately from results-page assertions.

Increase the assertion timeout for the search-input value check to at least 10 000 ms (or use the global `actionTimeout`) to tolerate any single-page-app re-render that may momentarily clear the field between navigation and assertion evaluation.

Add a dedicated assertion for the results list using a stable selector (e.g., `data-testid='product-card'` or `data-testid='search-results'`) with `toBeVisible()` and a `filter({ hasText: 'Desk Lamp' })` check, mirroring the validated UI state where 'Nova Desk Lamp' is confirmed present. This closes the coverage gap between the UI validation PASS and the script FAIL.

caseResults.failureCause: Error: Unable to resolve locator. selector="[data-testid='product-card']" description="Assert at least one product card is visible in the results"
caseResults.validationSummary: All validation criteria are met. The search input contains 'Desk Lamp', results are visible, at least one product card ('Nova Desk Lamp') is displayed, and no empty-state message is shown.
improvements: Replace the primary selector '[data-testid='product-card']' with a broader fallback chain that includes semantic and structural alternatives such as '.product-card', '[class*="product"]', '[class*="card"]', 'article', and 'li[class*="item"]' so the resolveLocator helper can find real DOM elements when data-testid attributes are absent

After search submission, add an explicit 'waitForLoadState("networkidle")' or 'waitForSelector' on the results container before attempting to resolve product card locators, preventing race conditions where assertions run before results render

Inject a page-inspection step that captures 'page.content()' or runs 'page.evaluate(() => document.body.innerHTML)' on failure to log the actual DOM structure, enabling faster diagnosis of missing data-testid attributes in future failures

Add a DOM-attribute audit step pre-test that checks whether '[data-testid="product-card"]' exists at all; if absent, emit a warning and automatically switch to the best structural fallback rather than throwing a hard error

Coordinate with the development team to enforce consistent application of 'data-testid' attributes on product card elements as part of the definition of done, eliminating selector brittleness at the source

Extend the fallback selector list inside resolveLocator to include text-based locators such as 'page.locator(":text('Desk Lamp')")' and 'page.getByRole("listitem")' as last-resort candidates when all CSS selectors fail

Since the validation result confirms the UI is correct (confidence 98, all checks PASS), mark this failure as a test-infrastructure defect rather than a product defect and add a regression guard: assert that at least one fallback selector resolves before the test is published to CI

Parameterise the search keyword ('Desk Lamp') as a test data variable and add a data-driven second case with a partial match (e.g., 'Lamp') to increase coverage of the search relevance acceptance criterion

caseResults.failureCause: Error: Unable to resolve locator. selector="canvas" description="Wait for the canvas element to be present and visible in the DOM"
caseResults.validationSummary: The page shows a GitHub Pages 404 error, meaning the drawing application was never loaded. No canvas, drawing tools, or interactive elements are present.
improvements: Add a pre-test URL health check step that performs an HTTP GET request to the target base URL and asserts a 2xx status code before proceeding; if the check fails, skip or abort the test with a descriptive message such as 'Application unreachable – verify BASE_URL configuration'

Externalize the base URL into a required environment variable (e.g., DRAWING_APP_URL) and validate its presence at the start of the test suite, throwing a clear configuration error if it is missing or pointing to a non-existent deployment

Replace the bare 'canvas' selector with a prioritized fallback selector chain: ['canvas#drawing-canvas', 'canvas[data-testid]', 'canvas'] and add a page.waitForLoadState('networkidle') call after navigation to ensure the SPA/application has fully initialized before attempting canvas resolution

Capture the full page URL and page title inside the resolveLocator error handler so that 404 or redirect scenarios are immediately identifiable in the failure log without needing to inspect the screenshot separately

Add an explicit assertion immediately after navigation – e.g., expect(page.url()).not.toContain('404') and await expect(page.locator('body')).not.toContainText('404') – so the test fails fast with a meaningful message rather than propagating to canvas interaction steps

Introduce a global setup fixture that verifies the drawing application is deployed and reachable before any test in the suite runs, preventing all dependent tests from executing against a broken environment
Document the correct application URL in a README or .env.example file and add a CI pipeline step that validates the deployment URL resolves successfully before triggering the Playwright test run

caseResults.failureCause: Error: Unable to resolve locator. selector="canvas" description="Wait for network idle to ensure SPA has fully initialized before canvas interaction"
caseResults.validationSummary: The screenshot shows a marketplace/e-commerce application, not a drawing application. There is no canvas element visible, and the app does not support drawing functionality as required by the user story.
improvements: Verify and correct the base URL in the Playwright configuration (playwright.config.js or .env) — the test is navigating to a marketplace/e-commerce app instead of a drawing application. The `baseURL` must point to the actual drawing tool URL (e.g., http://localhost:3000/draw) before any canvas interaction can succeed.

Add an explicit URL assertion at test start using `expect(page).toHaveURL(/draw|canvas|paint/i)` immediately after navigation to fail fast with a clear message if the wrong application is loaded, rather than failing silently at canvas lookup.

Replace the generic `canvas` selector fallback chain with a more resilient multi-strategy locator: try `canvas[id]`, `canvas[data-testid]`, `role=img` with canvas tag, and `document.querySelector('canvas')` via `page.evaluate()` — log which strategy succeeded to aid debugging.

Add a pre-condition guard that checks the page title or a known DOM landmark unique to the drawing app (e.g., `await expect(page.locator('[data-app="drawing"]')).toBeVisible()`) before proceeding, and skip/fail with a descriptive message if the wrong environment is detected.

Increase canvas wait timeout and use `page.waitForFunction(() => !!document.querySelector('canvas'))` with a 15-second timeout instead of relying solely on `networkidle`, since SPA canvas elements may mount after network activity settles.

After resolving the correct URL, implement pixel-level drawing verification: capture a screenshot of the canvas bounding box before and after the drag interaction using `page.evaluate()` to read `canvas.toDataURL()`, then assert that the two base64 strings differ to confirm a stroke was rendered.

Parameterize the application URL via environment variable `DRAWING_APP_URL` and add a CI/CD config validation step that asserts the variable is set and reachable before test execution begins, preventing wrong-environment test runs.

Add a diagnostic screenshot and `page.evaluate(() => document.documentElement.outerHTML.slice(0, 2000))` dump on canvas locator failure to expose the actual DOM structure in the error report, making root-cause analysis faster.

caseResults.failureCause: Error: Unable to resolve locator. selector="role=button[name='Login']" description="Wait for the Login button to be visible on the page"
caseResults.validationSummary: The screenshot shows a 404 GitHub Pages error page, not a login page. No login button is present, and none of the validation criteria can be satisfied.
improvements: Add a pre-flight URL validation step at the start of the test that asserts the page does not return a 404 or error state before proceeding, using `await expect(page).not.toHaveURL(/404/)` and `await expect(page.locator('body')).not.toContainText('404')` to fail fast with a meaningful message

Introduce a configurable BASE_URL environment variable (e.g., `process.env.BASE_URL`) and validate it is set and reachable before test execution, throwing a descriptive setup error if the target URL is unreachable or misconfigured

Expand the login button selector fallback chain to include multiple strategies: `role=button[name='Login']`, `role=button[name='Sign In']`, `[data-testid='login-button']`, `button[type='submit']`, `input[type='submit']`, and `text=Login` to handle varied implementations

Wrap the `page.goto()` call with a response status check: `const response = await page.goto(BASE_URL); if (!response || response.status() >= 400) throw new Error('Page load failed with status: ' + response?.status())` to catch 404/5xx errors immediately

Add a `waitForLoadState('networkidle')` or `waitForLoadState('domcontentloaded')` assertion after navigation before any element resolution to ensure the login page is fully rendered before selector resolution begins
Capture and attach the page URL and page title in the error context when `resolveLocator` fails, so diagnostics clearly show whether the wrong page was loaded rather than just a missing element

Create a smoke test fixture or setup hook that verifies the application login page is accessible and returns HTTP 200 before any TC-001 test logic executes, gating the entire suite on environment availability

Store the screenshot path from `.playwright-output` in the execution result and cross-reference it in the validation step to confirm the visible page matches the expected login page context before running UI assertions

caseResults.failureCause: Error: Unable to resolve locator. selector="css=button[type='submit']" description="Wait for a submit-type button to be visible on the page"
caseResults.validationSummary: The application page returns a 404 error from GitHub Pages, meaning the site does not exist. No login button is present, and none of the login-related criteria can be satisfied.
improvements: Validate the target URL is reachable and returns HTTP 200 before executing any test steps; add a pre-test network check using Playwright's `page.goto()` response status assertion: `const response = await page.goto(BASE_URL); expect(response.status()).toBe(200);` and fail fast with a clear message if the page returns 4xx/5xx.

Externalize the base URL into a `.env` file or Playwright `playwright.config.js` `use.baseURL` property so the correct environment URL is injected at runtime and never hardcoded to a broken GitHub Pages URL.

Expand the login button selector fallback chain to cover more real-world patterns before throwing: try in order — `button[type='submit']`, `input[type='submit']`, `button:has-text('Login')`, `button:has-text('Sign in')`, `[data-testid='login-btn']`, `a:has-text('Login')` — using `page.locator().first()` with `isVisible()` checks.

Add a page-load guard using `await expect(page).not.toHaveURL(/404/)` and `await expect(page.locator('body')).not.toContainText('404')` immediately after navigation to detect error pages early and surface a meaningful failure message instead of a selector resolution error.

Introduce a dedicated smoke-check test that verifies the environment URL resolves correctly and the login page renders expected landmarks (heading, username field, password field, submit button) before running the full TC-001 flow, so infrastructure failures are distinguished from functional failures.

Store environment-specific credentials (username, password) in Playwright's `process.env` or a secrets manager and reference them via `process.env.TEST_USERNAME` / `process.env.TEST_PASSWORD` to avoid hardcoded values and support multi-environment runs.

After a successful login click, add an explicit navigation assertion such as `await expect(page).toHaveURL(/dashboard|home/)` and a visible-element assertion on a post-login landmark (e.g., `await expect(page.locator('[data-testid="user-menu"]')).toBeVisible()`) to fully validate the acceptance criterion that the user is redirected to the dashboard.

caseResults.failureCause: Error: Unable to resolve locator. selector="role=main" description="Verify the main content area is visible"
caseResults.validationSummary: The page shows a GitHub Pages 404 error, meaning the site does not exist. All criteria related to content, search functionality, and valid results cannot be met.
improvements: Add a URL reachability pre-check at the start of the test: perform a page.goto() and assert response.status() === 200 before any UI interaction; if the check fails, skip the test with a clear message like 'Target URL returned 404 – environment not reachable' instead of propagating a misleading locator error

Introduce an environment validation fixture or global setup step that verifies the base URL is live and returns a non-error HTTP status before any test suite runs, preventing all downstream selector failures caused by a missing application

Replace the generic 'role=main' ARIA selector fallback chain with page-specific, DOM-verified selectors (e.g., data-testid, semantic landmarks confirmed in the actual deployed app); the current fallback list is resolving against a GitHub Pages 404 page that has no <main> element

Store the target base URL in a Playwright project configuration (playwright.config.js baseURL) and validate it against an allowed-environments list at test initialisation, throwing a descriptive setup error rather than a runtime locator error when the URL is wrong or unreachable

Capture and log the full page title and URL in the error context whenever resolveLocator() throws, so failure messages immediately surface '404 – GitHub Pages' instead of only 'Unable to resolve locator', reducing diagnosis time

Add a smoke guard step as the very first test action: assert that page.title() does not match known error patterns (e.g., /404|not found|error/i) and that page.url() matches the expected origin, failing fast with an environment-level error before any functional assertion is attempted

Parameterise test runs with a required ENV variable (e.g., BASE_URL) and gate CI execution on a prior health-check job that curls the URL; block test execution entirely if the health-check fails, keeping test results clean and meaningful

Update the resolveLocator fallback strategy to include at least three progressively broader selectors tied to the actual application's HTML structure (confirmed via screenshot analysis or DOM snapshot), and remove ARIA role selectors that are absent on error pages from the primary candidate list
